title: Pseudocystic appearance of an orbital carcinoid metastasis.
author_string: Halliday, Luke A, Curragh, David, Sia, Paul Ikgan, Selva, Dinesh
abstract: Carcinoid tumours are a low-grade neuroendocrine malignancy that infrequently metastisizes to orbital structures. The typical radiological appearance of carcinoid is a solid, well-circumscribed mass that enhances with contrast. We present a case of orbital carcinoid metastasis with pseudocystic radiological appearance and review of the literature.
